incidents.vector: Exploited a zero-day in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (CVE-2026-20131, CVSS 10) since January 26, weeks before public disclosure on March 4.
incidents.impact: Multi-stage intrusions with double extortion against multiple organizations including healthcare and government targets. Amazon discovered Interlock's misconfigured server exposing their full operational toolkit.

incidents.vector: Compromised Microsoft Intune administrative access to issue mass remote-wipe commands across the company's global device fleet.
incidents.impact: Over 200,000 devices wiped across offices in 79 countries. 5,000+ workers sent home in Ireland. Login pages defaced. FBI seized attacker domains and some group leaders reportedly killed in airstrikes.

incidents.vector: Unauthenticated remote code execution via CVE-2026-21992 (CVSS 9.8) in Oracle Identity Manager and Web Services Manager, prompting an emergency out-of-cycle patch.
incidents.impact: Critical RCE affecting 1,000+ organizations including major enterprises. Similar to a previously exploited vulnerability (CVE-2025-61757) already on CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.

incidents.victim: Mobile device users in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine
incidents.attacker: Russia — state-linked espionage group; commercial surveillance vendors
incidents.vector: Full-chain iOS exploit ('DarkSword') leveraging six zero-day vulnerabilities in iOS 18.4–18.7, deployed via phishing websites mimicking Snapchat.
incidents.impact: Three distinct malware families installed on targets' devices for surveillance. Used by at least one suspected Russian espionage group, a Turkish commercial surveillance vendor, and an unattributed actor. All vulnerabilities patched in iOS 26.3.

incidents.vector: Ransomware deployment resulting in a 9-day system outage affecting Mississippi's only Level I trauma center.
incidents.impact: 10,000-employee hospital shut down for 9 days (late Feb to Mar 2). $800,000 ransom demanded. Patient care severely disrupted at the state's sole top-level trauma facility.

incidents.vector: Ransomware attack on county government systems serving 600,000 residents.
incidents.impact: $800,000 ransom demanded. County government services disrupted for hundreds of thousands of residents.

incidents.vector: Four botnets (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad) hijacked 3 million+ IoT devices including 2 million Android TV boxes for DDoS-for-hire and extortion.
incidents.impact: Over 300,000 DDoS attacks launched, including a record 29.7 Tbps attack. DOJ, Canada, Germany, Netherlands, and Europol coordinated the takedown. Infrastructure seized.

incidents.victim: Emennet Pasargad, Integrity Technology Group, i-SOON (sanctioned entities)
incidents.attacker: Iran — IRGC (Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps); China — state-linked espionage groups
incidents.vector: EU imposed cyber sanctions against Iranian (IRGC-linked Emennet Pasargad) and Chinese (Integrity Technology / i-SOON) entities for campaigns including the Charlie Hebdo hack, Paris Olympics disruption, and global espionage.
incidents.impact: Asset freezes and travel bans imposed. Named entities linked to Raptor Train botnet operations and multi-year espionage campaigns. Co-founders of i-SOON personally sanctioned.

incidents.vector: Long-running campaign (since at least 2020) using custom backdoors (AppleChris, MemFun) and credential harvesters targeting domain controllers, web servers, and executive workstations.
incidents.impact: Military capability assessments, organizational structures, and details of collaboration with Western armed forces stolen. Campaign demonstrated years of 'strategic operational patience.'

incidents.victim: Telecom and government organizations across four continents
incidents.vector: Global cyber-espionage campaign ('GRIDTIDE') targeting telecommunications providers and government agencies across four continents.
incidents.impact: Campaign disrupted by Google/Mandiant. Scope and impact suggest a major state-sponsored operation targeting strategic communications infrastructure worldwide.

incidents.vector: Compromised npm package ('QUIETVAULT') used in a software supply chain attack that escalated to AWS administrator access within 72 hours of installation.
incidents.impact: Full AWS environment compromise achieved rapidly through a single malicious dependency. Highlights ongoing supply chain risks in the JavaScript ecosystem.

incidents.vector: DOJ seized four domains used by Iran's MOIS for psychological operations, transnational repression, and claiming credit for cyberattacks.
incidents.impact: Domains used to send death threats to Iranian dissidents, offer bounties, post PII of ~190 IDF personnel, and claim theft of 851 GB from a civilian community. FBI-wanted hackers reportedly killed in military strikes.

incidents.vector: Ransomware attack with data exfiltration of 219 GB including SSNs, driver's licenses, financial, and medical records.
incidents.impact: 235,000 individuals affected. $400,000 ransom demanded. Wisconsin's largest ambulance service with 750 employees had sensitive patient data exposed.

incidents.victim: UAE energy and marine services companies
incidents.vector: Four waves of attacks (Aug 2025–Feb 2026) using Rust-based backdoors (BlackBeard) and AI-generated code, targeting a company with ties to Saudi Aramco.
incidents.impact: Expanded targeting to maritime, aviation, and financial sectors across Israel, Hungary, Turkey, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Turkmenistan, Egypt, and South America.

incidents.vector: FBI and international partners dismantled the SocksEscort residential proxy botnet that infected home and small business routers, offering 369,000 IP addresses for rent.
incidents.impact: Network used for bank/crypto account takeovers, unemployment fraud, ransomware, and DDoS. Victims include a NYC crypto holder ($1M loss) and a PA manufacturer ($700K). Earned over EUR 5M.

incidents.vector: DOJ charged incident responder Angelo Martino with secretly feeding confidential victim information to ALPHV/BlackCat ransomware operators while negotiating ransoms.
incidents.impact: Martino earned $1.2M from one attack on a Florida medical company. Co-conspirators at two security firms already pled guilty. Ransom negotiations manipulated for amounts up to $26M.

incidents.vector: Weaponized Kubernetes infrastructure to steal cryptocurrency, exploiting cloud-native environments for persistent access.
incidents.impact: Billions in digital assets stolen by North Korean actors to fund WMD and ballistic missile programs. Treasury report confirms cryptocurrency theft remains a primary DPRK revenue source.

incidents.vector: Year-long undetected network intrusion beginning November 2024.
incidents.impact: 3.4 million patient records exposed during a compromise that went undetected for over a year.

incidents.victim: Ubiquiti UniFi Network users (88,000 exposed instances)
incidents.vector: Critical path-traversal vulnerability (CVE-2026-22557, CVSS 10) in Ubiquiti UniFi Network Application allowing unauthenticated account takeover.
incidents.impact: 88,000 internet-exposed instances at risk, one-third in the United States. Low attack complexity with no authentication required. Patch released.

incidents.vector: Europol-led takedown of the Tycoon 2FA phishing platform, which provided adversary-in-the-middle phishing kits capable of bypassing multi-factor authentication.
incidents.impact: Major phishing-as-a-service infrastructure dismantled. Platform was widely used by criminal actors to steal credentials and session tokens from enterprise targets.
